page: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server configured with an Intel Xeon 2388G processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz, with a dedicated CPU allocation and 16 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests scoring 2503.49 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 36.27 GB/sec. PassMark memory testing also ranks highly with a score of 3390.33. However, multi-core performance and database operations align with average mid-tier expectations, as shown by a PassMark CPU score of 23271.22 and Redis SET operations at 16.84 million operations per second. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-block memory bandwidth, where uncached read and write speeds drop to 36.93 GB/sec and 19.38 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density by combining a dedicated 16-core CPU with a substantial 128 GB memory footprint and a large 3.8 TB NVMe SSD. This hardware profile avoids virtualization overhead, making it suitable for workloads requiring direct hardware access, high-speed local storage, and strong single-threaded execution. Recommended workloads include relational databases, memory-intensive caching layers, static web serving, and localized data processing tasks.
description: The Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a dedicated bare metal server featuring an Intel Xeon 2388G processor with 16 physical cores running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD, offering a high-density resource profile without virtualization overhead. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, with high-performing single-threaded gzip compression and PassMark memory scores, though multi-core metrics and large-block uncached memory bandwidth represent notable performance tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, static web serving, and local data processing tasks requiring dedicated hardware.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server featuring a 16-core Intel Xeon 2388G CPU, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for dedicated database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-capacity NVMe storage, strong single-core performance, and 128 GB of DDR4 memory.

128 GB of DDR4 memory paired with a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD
Strong single-core CPU and high-performing memory benchmark results

Appropriate for relational databases, caching layers, and static web serving
